questions.title: Narrative arc for the docs page
questions.context: DOCS-01 requires the page to cover: delegate decision shape, propagation rules (abort/timeout/cost), concurrency, locality, trace embedding, with at least one worked example. Existing docs/developer-usage.md uses an API-first table (lines 39-47). The page can lead with concepts, with API surface, or with a worked scenario.

options.label: Concept-first
options.description: Open with the recursive-coordination mental model (what `delegate` is for, how children embed in the parent trace), then walk through propagation/concurrency/locality, then show a worked example at the end. Best for readers who haven't seen recursive coordination before. Risk: API surface buried.

options.label: API-first (matches developer-usage.md)
options.description: Open with `delegate` decision shape and `RunCallOptions.maxDepth` table, then propagation rules in subsections, then example. Consistent with existing docs voice. Risk: assumes readers already understand WHY they would delegate.

options.label: Scenario-first (worked example up top)
options.description: Lead with a runnable example (coordinator plans → delegates broadcast → synthesizes), THEN unpack each surface (delegate, parentRunIds, propagation, locality). Mirrors how the paper presents the protocol. Risk: requires the reader to scroll back for definitions.

options.label: Hybrid: short concept blurb → API table → progressive worked example
options.description: Two-paragraph concept opener, an API surface table (`delegate`, `subRun.*`, `locality`, `maxConcurrentChildren`, `maxDepth`), then a worked example that grows in complexity (pile → stream → failure handling). Most navigable; longest page.

questions.title: Worked example placement and count
questions.context: DOCS-01 says 'at least one worked example.' Phase 4 D-XX decisions added several public-surface concepts (parentRunIds chain, structured failures, partial trace). Each concept could ride one big example or get its own snippet.

options.label: Single end-to-end example at the bottom
options.description: One coordinator-delegates-broadcast scenario, walked through start to finish with output. Reader sees one canonical shape. Risk: long block; harder to skim.

options.label: Multiple small fragments interleaved with concepts
options.description: Each subsection has a 5-15 line fragment showing just that concept (e.g., propagation section shows abort + clamp event, concurrency section shows maxConcurrentChildren). No single end-to-end. Risk: no canonical 'what does the trace look like' moment.

options.label: Both — fragments per concept + one canonical end-to-end at the end
options.description: Each subsection has its focused fragment; the page closes with a complete worked example exercising every surface. Best comprehension, longest page.

options.label: Two examples: a minimal pile() and a stream() that demuxes parentRunIds
options.description: Two complete examples showing the two API entry points; concepts referenced inline in each. Strong for the parent/child demux story; light on propagation/concurrency depth.

questions.title: Page length / split strategy
questions.context: developer-usage.md is 575 lines. The recursive-coordination surface is large (delegate, subRun.*, locality, maxConcurrentChildren, maxDepth, parentRunIds chain, structured failures, partial trace, replay parity).

options.label: Single focused page (~300-450 lines)
options.description: Concept + API + one example. Defer deep API reference to inline JSDoc / docs/reference.md. Easiest to maintain; risk of underexposing edge cases.

options.label: Single comprehensive page (~700-900 lines)
options.description: Cover every concept, every event variant, every error shape, replay parity, locality clamp, depth overflow. Single source of truth. Risk: long, but discoverable via TOC.

options.label: Split: concepts + API reference pages
options.description: `recursive-coordination.md` = concepts + worked example; new `recursive-coordination-reference.md` = exhaustive event/error/option tables. Adds public-surface page count (more files in `docs/`).

questions.title: How prominently to feature the parentRunIds chain demux pattern
questions.context: Phase 4 D-02 adds `parentRunIds: readonly string[]` to every event flowing through a parent stream. Demux pattern: `event.parentRunIds?.[event.parentRunIds.length - 1] === handle.runId`. This is the SDK-facing change most stream consumers will hit.

options.label: Dedicated subsection with snippet + ASCII diagram
options.description: Show a parent → child → grandchild tree with chain values at each level; show the immediate-parent vs ancestry demux idioms. Maximum clarity for stream consumers.

options.label: Inline mention in the streaming example
options.description: Reference parentRunIds where stream() output is shown; one-line demux idiom; no diagram. Lighter touch.

options.label: Brief callout + link to event-schema.test.ts
options.description: One paragraph, point readers at the test for the canonical contract. Minimum doc surface; assumes readers will read tests.

questions.title: Document the structured `failures` block in coordinator prompts
questions.context: Phase 4 D-07 adds a structured `failures: ReadonlyArray<{ childRunId, code, message, partialCost }>` section to coordinator plan-turn prompts (in addition to the legacy `[sub-run X failed]:` transcript line). This affects how coordinator agents reason about retries.

options.label: Yes — dedicated subsection with the prompt template excerpt
options.description: Show the exact section a coordinator agent will see; lock the format readers can rely on.

options.label: Brief mention with link to coordinator.ts source
options.description: One paragraph noting the surface exists; readers grep the code for the exact shape.

options.label: Skip — this is internal prompt machinery, not public surface
options.description: Document only the trace-side `sub-run-failed` event + partialCost. Don't lock the prompt template.

questions.title: Out-of-scope / deferral callouts on the docs page
questions.context: PROJECT.md milestone goal: 'Agent-driven nesting only — caller-defined trees (Dogpile.nest) are deferred.' The docs page can name these explicitly or leave them unmentioned.

options.label: Explicit 'Not in v0.4.0' section
options.description: List: caller-defined trees (`Dogpile.nest`), worker delegation (only coordinator plan-turn delegates), final-synthesis-turn delegation. Sets reader expectations; preempts issues.

options.label: Inline notes only where relevant (e.g., 'plan turn only')
options.description: Mention the limit at the spot it matters (delegate parsing rejects worker delegation), don't enumerate. Less explicit; less wall of caveats.

options.label: No callouts — let the API surface speak
options.description: If it's not in the docs, it's not supported. Cleanest reading experience; relies on errors to teach the boundary.

questions.title: Example scenario — what mission does it actually solve?
questions.context: DOCS-02 requires `examples/recursive-coordination/` runnable against `createOpenAICompatibleProvider` exercising a real `delegate` flow end-to-end. The existing example (`examples/huggingface-upload-gui`) plans an upload GUI across all four protocols. The recursive example needs to demonstrate `delegate` specifically.

options.label: Research-and-synthesize
options.description: Top-level coordinator decomposes a research question, delegates a `broadcast` of researchers (one per angle), then a `sequential` synthesis pass. Shows fan-out + sequential consolidation. Familiar shape; matches paper-style multi-agent.

options.label: Code review fan-out
options.description: Coordinator delegates parallel reviewers (security/perf/style) via `broadcast`, escalates failures, synthesizes verdict. Concrete tech scenario; shows error escalation cleanly.

options.label: Plan-then-build (recursive coordinator)
options.description: Coordinator plans a feature, delegates a sub-`coordinator` for one sub-task that itself delegates. Demonstrates true recursion (depth ≥ 2) and `recursive: true` flag on sub-run-started. Strongest demo of the milestone's headline feature.

options.label: Mirror huggingface-upload-gui — same prompt, coordinator-with-delegate variant
options.description: Reuse the existing example's mission (Hugging Face upload GUI plan), wrap it in a coordinator that delegates each sub-step. Continuity with prior example; risk of duplication.

options.label: Generic 'plan/dispatch/synthesize' demo
options.description: Synthetic minimal scenario optimized to exercise every surface (delegate, propagation, concurrency, locality, parentRunIds, failure escalation). Best surface coverage; least story.

questions.title: Provider configuration in the example
questions.context: The huggingface example (examples/README.md lines 11-26) defaults to a local deterministic provider and opts into OpenAI-compatible via env vars (`DOGPILE_EXAMPLE_PROVIDER=openai-compatible`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`). DOCS-02 explicitly requires the example to be 'wired against `createOpenAICompatibleProvider`'.

options.label: Mirror huggingface — deterministic default + opt-in OpenAI-compatible
options.description: Repeatable without network/keys; DOCS-02 'wired against' satisfied by env-var path. Maximum parity with existing example.

options.label: Live-only (createOpenAICompatibleProvider as the only path)
options.description: Strict reading of DOCS-02. Simpler example; requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` to run anything. Risk: no offline reproduction; CI smoke can't run it.

options.label: Deterministic default + documented live-mode in README
options.description: Default deterministic; README shows the live env-var snippet. DOCS-02 is satisfied by 'wired against' (the code path exists), even if default is deterministic. Same as (a) but with cleaner README phrasing.

options.label: Two files: `run-deterministic.mjs` and `run-live.mjs`
options.description: Explicit split; readers pick. Verbose; doubles file count.

questions.title: Example package shape
questions.context: huggingface-upload-gui is a single `.mjs` script run from repo root after `pnpm run build` — no package.json. The repo's `files` allowlist excludes examples (they're repo-only). The recursive example could match or upgrade.

options.label: Single .mjs script (matches huggingface)
options.description: Run from repo root via `node examples/recursive-coordination/run.mjs`. Zero new package surface. Imports use `@dogpile/sdk` from built `dist/`.

options.label: Proper package with its own package.json
options.description: Adds a `package.json` declaring `@dogpile/sdk` as a dep (workspace ref). Closer to how a real consumer would shape things; adds workspace complexity.

options.label: TypeScript file run via tsx
options.description: Shows TS-first usage; matches typical consumer reality. Adds tsx as an example-only dev dep. Risk: more setup than the existing example.

questions.title: Which API entry point(s) does the example demonstrate?
questions.context: Public surface: `Dogpile.pile()` (single mission, returns result), `Dogpile.stream()` (live event stream + final result). Phase 4's parentRunIds chain only matters when streaming.

options.label: stream() — surfaces bubbled child events live
options.description: Best demo of parentRunIds demux + live trace. Output more interesting (event log). Slightly more code.

options.label: pile() — clean result.trace at the end
options.description: Shorter; shows replay-friendly artifact. Doesn't surface bubbled events visually.

options.label: Both — stream() example + a pile() comparison
options.description: Best coverage; longest example. Risk: scope creep (DOCS-02 says 'a delegate flow,' singular).

questions.title: Demonstrate failure escalation explicitly?
questions.context: Phase 4 D-06/D-07 cover sub-run failure handling: synthetic `sub-run-failed` events with `partialTrace` and `partialCost`, plus structured `failures` block in the coordinator prompt. The example can show happy path only or include an intentional failure.

options.label: Yes — include a child that intentionally fails (e.g., budget exhaustion)
options.description: Shows partial-trace shape, structured failures, retry decision in coordinator. Strongest demo of the propagation work.

options.label: Happy path only — failure handling is in the docs page
options.description: Keeps the example readable; failure scenarios ride the docs page. DOCS-02 doesn't require failure path.

options.label: Optional flag (`--simulate-failure`)
options.description: Run defaults to happy path; flag adds the failure variant. Explicit, but adds CLI flag plumbing.

questions.title: Demonstrate locality auto-clamp?
questions.context: Phase 3 D-XX: providers with `locality: 'local'` auto-clamp `maxConcurrentChildren` to 1; emits `sub-run-concurrency-clamped`. Pure visibility win; not required by DOCS-02 (which only mandates a delegate flow).

options.label: Yes — show a local provider with the clamp event in output
options.description: Demonstrates a phase-3 surface that's otherwise invisible to readers. Adds setup.

options.label: Comment-only — note the clamp behavior in code without exercising it
options.description: Surfaces the option without runtime path; lighter touch.

options.label: Skip — covered in the docs page only
options.description: Example focuses on delegate + propagation; locality stays in the page. Cleanest example scope.

questions.title: README 'Choose Your Path' row wording
questions.context: Existing rows (README.md lines 67-75) follow `<need>` → `<entry-point-snippet>`. DOCS-03 requires a row pointing at `delegate` / recursive coordination.

options.label: 'Run nested missions' → 'Coordinator agent emits `{ type: "delegate", ... }`'
options.description: Need-focused. Action verb in left column matches sibling rows. Right column shows the decision shape verbatim.

options.label: 'Dispatch sub-missions from a coordinator' → 'Use `delegate` decisions; see `docs/recursive-coordination.md`'
options.description: Says 'sub-mission' explicitly (matches PROJECT.md vocabulary). Right column points at docs page rather than showing code.

options.label: 'Recursive coordination' → 'Coordinator → `delegate` → child run; see `docs/recursive-coordination.md`'
options.description: Matches feature name. Right column compact + arrow notation. Mostly points at docs.

options.label: 'Run a coordinator that fans out into other Dogpile runs' → '`delegate` decision; `docs/recursive-coordination.md`'
options.description: Most descriptive left column; mirrors how a developer would Google this.

questions.title: Where does the new row go in the table?
questions.context: README 'Choose Your Path' table currently has 6 rows (lines 69-75) ordered roughly: pile → stream → protocol → engine → replay → openai-compat.

options.label: Append at the end (after openai-compat)
options.description: Lowest churn. Implies recursive coordination is a 'see also' rather than a top-line use case.

options.label: Insert after 'Compare coordination strategies'
options.description: Groups protocol-related concerns together. Natural reading flow: protocols → recursion.

options.label: Insert after 'Reuse fixed settings' (engine row)
options.description: Recursive coordination uses createEngine's surfaces (maxDepth, defaultSubRunTimeoutMs). Conceptually paired.

options.label: Insert second (right after pile)
options.description: Front-load the milestone's headline feature. Highest visibility; risks pushing the foundational rows down.

questions.title: examples/README.md update shape
questions.context: examples/README.md currently has one section (Hugging Face Upload GUI Plans) with its own header, run command, env-var table, and artifact path note.

options.label: Full subsection mirroring huggingface (header + run + env vars + artifact dir)
options.description: Symmetrical with existing entry. Self-contained for readers who land on examples/.

options.label: Minimal subsection (header + one-line description + run command)
options.description: Lighter; defers detail to docs/recursive-coordination.md and the example's own README if it has one.

options.label: One-line link only
options.description: Just '`examples/recursive-coordination/` — see `docs/recursive-coordination.md`'. Minimum surface.

questions.title: v0.4.0 changelog structure
questions.context: CHANGELOG.md currently has v0.4.0 grouped by phase: top-level Breaking + Added (Phase 1), then `### Added — ... (Phase 2)`, `(Phase 3)`. Phase 4 entries are not yet written. DOCS-04 requires ALL public-surface additions listed: `delegate` decision variant, `subRun.*` events, `locality` field, `maxConcurrentChildren` config, `maxDepth` config (and Phase 4 additions).

options.label: Keep phase-grouped (existing structure)
options.description: Add Phase 4 + Phase 5 sections. Lowest churn; preserves history of how the milestone landed. Risk: readers don't think in 'phases.'

options.label: Flatten to thematic groups (Breaking, Added — delegate, Added — propagation, Added — locality/concurrency, Added — streaming/errors, Added — docs)
options.description: Reader-focused; matches how npm consumers think. Loses phase tags. Significant rewrite of existing entries.

options.label: Hybrid: thematic top-level with phase tags inline
options.description: Themed sections with `(Phase N)` annotations preserved. Best of both; moderate rewrite.

options.label: Flat 'Breaking + Added' lists with NO phase grouping at all
options.description: Standard Keep-A-Changelog shape. Cleanest for npm readers. Largest rewrite of existing entries.

questions.title: AgentDecision breaking-change migration callout
questions.context: CHANGELOG.md line 5 already documents the AgentDecision discriminated-union breaking change ('Consumers must narrow on decision.type === participate before reading paper-style fields'). v0.3.x → v0.4.0 callers reading those fields directly will break.

options.label: Dedicated 'Migration' subsection with before/after code snippet
options.description: 10-20 line code block showing v0.3 vs v0.4 narrowing. Most user-friendly; anchors a docs link.

options.label: Keep current inline note in Breaking section, add a link to docs
options.description: Existing wording is technically complete. Add `→ see docs/recursive-coordination.md#agentdecision-narrowing`. Low churn.

options.label: Inline before/after snippet in the existing Breaking entry
options.description: Same Breaking entry, expanded with a fenced TS block. No new section header.

questions.title: Release timing — date-stamp v0.4.0 in this phase?
questions.context: CHANGELOG.md heading is currently `## [Unreleased] — v0.4.0`. The release gate (`pnpm run verify`) and `npm publish` flow is separate from documentation. PROJECT.md does not list a release task.

options.label: Date-stamp in this phase: `## [0.4.0] — 2026-05-01`
options.description: Phase 5 ships the version. Phase 5 includes the actual `npm publish` / git tag step. Strongest 'shipped' signal.

options.label: Leave as `[Unreleased]` — release is a separate concern
options.description: Phase 5 = docs only. A subsequent action (manual release) flips Unreleased → 0.4.0 with the date. Cleanest scope split.

options.label: Date-stamp the heading but DO NOT publish in this phase
options.description: Heading reads `## [0.4.0] — <release-target-date>` even before npm publish. Risk: the date is wrong if publish slips.

questions.title: Update docs/developer-usage.md?
questions.context: developer-usage.md is the long-form usage guide (575 lines). It does not currently mention `delegate`, `sub-run`, or recursive coordination. New readers may land here first via the README link.

options.label: Add a 'Recursive coordination' section (~50-80 lines) summarizing + linking to docs/recursive-coordination.md
options.description: Keeps developer-usage discoverability; avoids duplicating the full surface. Maintenance: two places to update.

options.label: Add a one-paragraph 'See also' callout near the coordinator section
options.description: Light touch; minimal duplication. Risk: readers in developer-usage may miss it.

options.label: Leave developer-usage alone
options.description: Single source of truth in the new docs page. Cleanest content split. Risk: readers in developer-usage don't discover the new surface.

questions.title: AGENTS.md / docs/reference.md / CLAUDE.md sync
questions.context: CLAUDE.md says 'AGENTS.md — repository guidelines (overlaps with this file; keep them consistent).' docs/reference.md lists exports. CLAUDE.md itself currently has no recursive-coordination text.

options.label: Update all three (AGENTS.md, docs/reference.md, CLAUDE.md)
options.description: Maintains stated invariants. Largest surface; most maintenance.

options.label: Update AGENTS.md + docs/reference.md only
options.description: AGENTS mirrors CLAUDE; reference.md mirrors exports. Skip CLAUDE.md (it's project-internal AI guidance, the recursive surface lives in docs/).

options.label: Update docs/reference.md only
options.description: Reference.md is the export catalog — must stay accurate. AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md left as-is unless they actively mislead.

options.label: Update none — recursive-coordination.md is the canonical home
options.description: Phase 5 strictly delivers what ROADMAP.md scopes. Cross-doc sweep happens in a follow-up.
